title: D'AMBROSIO'S
subtitle: the floating palace of fusion confusion · Vol 5 · The Empire
notes: Dante D'Ambrosio's riverboat restaurant. Moored to the same piling for nine years on the muddy divide between Texas and Louisiana. Sixty years of sweat and deals done in shadows thick as gumbo gravy, all to build this — this floating palace of fusion confusion. The name plastered on the side in gaudy, vaguely Italianate script that is, Dante is almost certain, peeling. He has not gone outside to look in eight months; signage is a job for younger men. The boat has two faces. The upper deck and main deck are the public face: the brass railing Dante installed in the second year, the leaded window the architect insisted on (overlooking the dining room from the helm), the brunch crowd in their Sunday clothes, the corner booth where Quentin Paul holds court. The lower deck is the empire under the empire: the catering office that books wedding gigs across three parishes; the card room where a tightly-curated set of regulars plays Saturday nights; the back room where the high-stakes table runs by invitation; the staff locker room where coats and personal effects live and the back stair lets people leave by the route they came in. Seventeen spaces across three decks. Three thresholds (GANGWAY out the public way, SIDE DOOR river-facing for the discreet exit, STAFF EXIT off the back for the people who came in through the back). This board is shared canonically by the Empress (Nicola the hostess), the Emperor (Dante at the helm), and one Hierophant scenario (Paul at Table 17). Each arcana's scenarios reframe it.
ambient_pool: the boat groans. it does not, technically, list. dante checks the levels with his father's spirit level once a month.
the brass railing dante installed in the second year is bright in a way the boat does not, otherwise, manage.
from the helm, through the leaded window, the dining room sounds like a dining room.
the muddy divide between texas and louisiana is outside every river-side window.
the bell above the front door rings the way it rang in 1962. dante's father did not replace it. dante has not replaced it.
downstairs, the catering office phone rings. somebody in beaumont needs three hundred plates for a saturday in october.
in the back room the door is closed. it has been closed for forty minutes. somebody is winning.

spaces.label: HELM
spaces.flavor: dante's office at the helm · the desk, the file cabinet with the bottle, the leaded window overlooking the dining room, the photograph of his wife not replaced in twenty-three years, the brass clock from his father's office.
arrival_lines: the office is the office. the carpet under the route between desk and window has the path worn into it.
the leaded window shows the dining room as a slightly-waved aquarium. the helm has its own air.

spaces.label: OFFICE STAIRCASE
spaces.flavor: the iron stair down from the helm to the dining room · narrow, rusted in the way iron stairs in louisiana are rusted · the architect's pride · the only route between dante's office and the rest of the boat.
arrival_lines: the stair is the stair. each step creaks at a slightly different pitch.
from halfway down you can see the dining room and the bar at once. dante uses this angle on busy nights.

spaces.label: MAÎTRE D' STAND
spaces.flavor: the hostess stand at the entrance · the reservation book, the pencil with the eraser worn down, the candy dish from 1971 nobody has ever fully emptied · nicola's post on a friday night.
arrival_lines: the bell above the front door is at head height. it rings when it has to ring.
the candy dish has the same hard candies that have been in it since dante's father. they are, somehow, still good.

spaces.label: MAIN DINING ROOM
spaces.flavor: the brunch floor · twelve tables · the chairs in a particular not-quite-uniform that nicola straightens between seatings · the lights at a level dante set in '98 and has not adjusted.
arrival_lines: the room registers entries. the laughter at table 12 changes pitch when a regular walks in.
from this angle the leaded window in the helm is visible above the bar. dante is, sometimes, behind it.

spaces.label: PRIVATE DINING
spaces.flavor: the fine dining partition · twelve covers maximum · prix fixe by reservation only · the room dante added in '04 because alberto told him the future of the boat was higher margin per cover.
arrival_lines: the partition is mahogany. it was salvaged from a hotel in mobile that closed in '01.
the table in the center seats six. it has been set for six since the last booking, three weeks ago.

spaces.label: TABLE 17
spaces.flavor: the corner booth · overlooking the muddy divide between texas and louisiana through the boat's deepest window · where quentin paul holds court on sundays · gloria has not, in eleven years, looked at it when paul is speaking from it.
arrival_lines: the booth is the booth. the leather is creased in the shape of the people who use it most.
the window beside the booth shows the river. the river does not, this hour, look back.

spaces.label: SAMMY'S BAR
spaces.flavor: the bar at the far end of the dining room · sixteen stools · sammy behind it since the boat opened in '83 · sixty-one years old · the kind of long-suffering catholic loyalty dante has both relied on and quietly mocked.
arrival_lines: sammy is pouring. he does not look up. he registers who has arrived without looking up.
the brass rail along the bar is the bright brass dante is maintaining; the warm-brass railing in the helm is the wrong brass.

spaces.label: THE PASS
spaces.flavor: the pass between bar and kitchen · hector hands plates through · the heat lamp · the ticket spike with the dinner orders in the order they came in.
arrival_lines: the heat lamp warms the air above the pass to a noticeable degree.
hector at the line through the gap. he sees you. depending on the day, he crosses himself or does not.

spaces.label: KITCHEN
spaces.flavor: hector's line · two stations · the hollandaise that breaks if you look at it wrong · the walk-in to the left, the dish pit to the right · the radio on a milk crate (spanish, sometimes salsa).
arrival_lines: the kitchen is the kitchen. the heat is the second heat behind the heat in the dining room.
hector is at saute. the prep cook is at the salad station. the radio is on the kind of station the radio is on.

spaces.label: BACK CORRIDOR
spaces.flavor: the corridor behind the kitchen · the time clock, the coat rack, the bulletin board with the schedule · the door at the end opens to the side door.
arrival_lines: the corridor smells like the kitchen wants it to smell.
the schedule on the bulletin board has nicola's name on it for friday, saturday, sunday brunch.

spaces.label: SIDE DOOR
spaces.flavor: the river-facing door · the discreet exit · the door dean used in '24 when he left after sitting at table 14 for two hours without ordering.
arrival_lines: the side door opens onto the small lower-deck walkway directly above the water.
the door has a sign nobody reads: STAFF ONLY. it is also the door dean used. the sign is, in some way, a joke.

spaces.label: CATERING OFFICE
spaces.flavor: the catering office on the lower deck · the desk dante manages the events business from · the phone ringing every fifteen minutes about a saturday wedding in october · the filing cabinet with eight years of contracts.
arrival_lines: the office is windowless. the only light is the desk lamp dante turned on at three.
the contracts on the desk are for a three-hundred-plate wedding in port arthur. the bride's father is somebody dante did business with in '88.

spaces.label: THE CARD ROOM
spaces.flavor: the card room · saturday-night five-card stud · a tightly curated set of regulars dante's father vetted in the seventies and dante has not, in thirty years, replaced more than half of · the green felt is original.
arrival_lines: the green felt is, in the wear pattern, a map of the elbows of men who have not been to this room in twenty years.
the chips are in their racks. the deck is in the box. the room is between sessions.

spaces.label: THE BACK ROOM
spaces.flavor: the high-stakes table · by invitation only · the door does not, ever, have a sign · paul has been once. dante's father played here. dante does not, on principle, play.
arrival_lines: the door is closed. it has been closed for forty minutes. somebody is winning, on the other side of it.
the room behind the door is the room behind the door. you do not enter without an introduction.

spaces.label: STAFF LOCKER ROOM
spaces.flavor: the lockers · gloria's, hector's, the prep cook's, the dishwasher's, the bus kid's, sammy's at the end with his name plate from '83 · the bench where staff change shoes.
arrival_lines: the lockers smell like the lockers. gloria's has a small framed photo of her grandmother taped inside.
the bench is worn smooth in the places where shifts have changed for forty years.

spaces.label: STAFF EXIT
spaces.flavor: the back of the lower deck · onto the small back walkway · how staff disembark · how the card-room regulars sometimes leave when they have decided not to use the gangway.
arrival_lines: the staff exit opens onto a narrow walkway that runs to the back of the lot. the lot ends in chain link.
from here you can see the parking lot but not the gangway. that is the point of it being back here.

spaces.label: GANGWAY
spaces.flavor: the plank from boat to dock · the public way on, the public way off · the route most guests take · the route nobody who has been to the card room takes after.
arrival_lines: the gangway flexes when the river does. you stopped noticing in 2009.
the parking lot is on the other side. dante's security camera from '03 covers the gangway and the first ten feet of the lot. there is a known shadow at the dumpster the camera does not cover.

bloom_track.label: THE FLOOR HOLDS
bloom_track.notes: Generic positive doom-clock for the boat. Engine variable: _bloom (used by Empress scenarios) or _ledger (used by Emperor scenarios) — depending on which arcana's scenario is running. The semantic is the same: the boat held its weight tonight. The brunch hit its peak and came back down without anybody walking out. The card room ran clean. The catering shipment for the saturday wedding got prepped without a vendor calling about a lien. Threshold means the night made the night it was supposed to be.

ladder.label: the boat is the boat. the brass railing is the wrong-bright brass.
the first table is seated. the first card hand is dealt.
the second wave of brunchers is at the gangway.
halfway · sammy's bar is at its sunday volume.
the catering office has confirmed the saturday wedding.
one more thing and the night is the night.
the floor held. the boat held. dante closes the helm light.

ledger_track.label: THE LEDGER CLOSED
ledger_track.notes: Aliased positive doom-clock for Emperor scenarios. Same engine slot as the bloom_track semantically (the engine reads _ledger for emperor arcana_id, _bloom for empress). The ladder above is shared in flavor.

ladder.label: the ledger is open to today's date.
two entries. the day is starting to look like a day.
halfway · the catering side is balanced.
the card-room weekend take is logged.
one more entry and the ledger is the ledger.
the ledger closed. dante sets the pen down.
